summary: Session 1: probed the installed GitHub Copilot CLI against the six-point go/no-go gate on the operator's personal seat (3 provider families confirmed dispatchable; deterministic JSONL/stderr separation; usage visibility characterized as premium-request-count only; the CLI's silent auto-update mid-session surfaced a new --no-auto-update design requirement); routed the architecture design lock (lockfile schema, role/alias mapping, timeouts, retryable-error taxonomy, breaker default=200, guard-exclusion list); saved s1-cli-contract.md and s1-design-adjudication.md and folded the lock into spec.md. Cross-provider verification (GPT-5.4) raised two Major findings about gate rigor (missing target-team-seat/GitHub-Models checks; an unsanctioned GO-WITH-OPEN-ITEMS verdict); escalated to the operator, who explicitly overrode both and authorized Sessions 2-5 to proceed on single-seat evidence. No pivot to GitHub Models.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (task_type=analysis) recommended switching from Claude Code to the OpenAI/Codex binding for Session 2 because the work profile shifts from architectural reasoning (Session 1, locked) to implementation-heavy code generation and a fake-spawner pytest suite against a fixed design, which the analysis judged plays to a GPT-series model's strengths; high effort given the multi-module state-machine implementation and full test coverage required.
